message.content: Adversaries may exploit Direct Volume Access to bypass file access controls and file system monitoring on Windows systems. This technique allows programs to directly interact with logical volumes, enabling them to read and write files by analyzing file system data structures. By circumventing standard file access controls and monitoring tools, attackers can stealthily manipulate or exfiltrate data. Commonly, utilities like NinjaCopy in PowerShell are used to facilitate these actions. The potential impact includes unauthorized data access, data manipulation, and evasion of detection mechanisms. Mitigation strategies involve restricting administrative privileges, monitoring for unusual volume access patterns, and employing endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to identify and block suspicious activities.
